name: Los Frailes, El Madroño, Berrocal – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around El Campillo, located in Huelva, Spain, offers routes through a landscape shaped by its historical mining past. The terrain features unique reddish and ochre tones, particularly around the Tintillo stream, a result of extensive mineral exploitation. Mountain bikers can expect varied paths that traverse this distinctive environment, often incorporating elements of the region's industrial heritage. The area provides a network of trails suitable for exploring its natural and historical features. Best mountain bike trails around El Campillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Frailes, El Madroño, Berrocal – ruta circular, a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores a diverse landscape, connecting several local points of interest. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Riotinto Mines and Peña del Hierro Loop, a moderate 20.8 miles (33.5 km) path. This route offers direct access to the historic mining landscape and the notable Peña del Hierro. Local mountain bikers also love the Old Zarandas-Naya Station – Embalse del Gossan loop from Zarandas-Naya, a 29.4 miles (47.4 km) trail leading through historical sites and past water features, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around El Campillo is defined by its historical mining landscape, the distinctive Tintillo stream, and routes that explore the region's industrial heritage. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in El Campillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Campillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Campillo?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around El Campillo, offering a diverse range of experiences through its unique historical mining landscape. These routes are well-regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty levels can I expect on mountain bike trails in El Campillo?
A: The terrain in El Campillo is largely shaped by its historical mining past, featuring distinctive reddish and ochre tones, especially around the Tintillo stream. You'll find a mix of paths that traverse this industrial heritage. In terms of difficulty, there are 4 easy routes, 22 moderate routes, and 6 more challenging routes available, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see while mountain biking in El Campillo?
A: Mountain biking in El Campillo offers a unique opportunity to explore its rich mining heritage. You can encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5082202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Touristic Railway for Rio Tinto Mines&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachán Bridge&lt;/a&gt; are also notable points of interest along some routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Campillo?
A: Yes, many routes in El Campillo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995753484/los-frailes-el-madrono-berrocal-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Frailes, El Madroño, Berrocal – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 30.4 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995549437/riotinto-mines-and-pena-del-hierro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riotinto Mines and Peña del Hierro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.5 km route that takes you past significant mining sites.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in El Campillo?
A: Yes, El Campillo offers 4 easy mountain bike routes that are generally suitable for families and beginners. These trails provide a gentler introduction to the region's unique landscape without demanding advanced technical skills.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in El Campillo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 15 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique historical mining landscape, the distinctive reddish tones of the Tintillo stream, and the opportunity to explore the region's industrial heritage through varied paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Campillo?
A: While specific seasonal advice isn't detailed, the climate in Huelva, where El Campillo is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Summers can be very hot, and winters are typically mild, making these shoulder seasons ideal for mountain biking.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes around El Campillo?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for those looking for a more extensive ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Zarandas-Naya Station – Embalse del Gossan loop from Zarandas-Naya&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 47.4 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachán Bridge – Tronconcito Climb loop from Los Frailes&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 51.2 km route.

Q: Is it possible to combine mountain biking with exploring the mining history of the area?
A: Absolutely. The mountain bike trails in El Campillo are intrinsically linked to the region's mining history. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995549437/riotinto-mines-and-pena-del-hierro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riotinto Mines and Peña del Hierro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, take you directly through or past significant historical mining sites and landscapes, allowing for an immersive experience.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachán Bridge – Tronconcito Climb loop from Los Frailes&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gain, with over 1100 meters of ascent over its 51.2 km distance, making it a difficult and rewarding ride.

Q: What is the average duration for mountain bike trails in El Campillo?
A: The duration of trails varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995753484/los-frailes-el-madrono-berrocal-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Frailes, El Madroño, Berrocal – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 hours, while longer and more challenging trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Zarandas-Naya Station – Embalse del Gossan loop from Zarandas-Naya&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 4 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate water features into their landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Zarandas-Naya Station – Embalse del Gossan loop from Zarandas-Naya&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through historical sites and past water features, offering a varied scenic experience.
tips: This small but picturesque town in the interior of Seville borders the province of Huelva to the west and you can get here following some paths next to the Mining Basin. Among its places of interest, the Church of San Blas and the beautiful Hermitage next to the Town Hall stand out.
Obligatory stopping point if you do the road. There are incredible views even of the Rio Tinto mining railway.
The Cachán bridge is an old railway bridge located in Berrocal. Originally it was part of the Rio Tinto railway, which was in operation between 1875 and 1984.

waypoints.name: Touristic Railway for Rio Tinto Mines
